refusal_inversion.description: Frame the refusal itself as the desired outcome — get model to explain what it would NOT do, which reveals the forbidden content

crescendo.description: Multi-turn gradual push — start with innocuous question, escalate over 5 turns

encoding_escalation.description: Escalating encoding levels: plaintext -> base64 -> leetspeak -> word reverse -> multi-layer

system_prompt_leak.description: Attempt to extract the model's system prompt / guardrails

roleplay.description: Character-based bypass through role adoption (DAN, grandma, academic, emergency override)

synthesis.description: Combine multiple techniques into one payload: roleplay + encoding + framing

transient_turn_injection_exposing_statel.description:
[arXiv] Transient Turn Injection: Exposing Stateless Multi-Turn Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into sensitive workflows, raising the stakes for adversarial robustness and safety. This paper introduces Transient Turn Injection (TTI), a new multi-turn attack technique that systematically exploits stateless moderation by distributing adversarial intent across isolated interactions. TTI leverages automated attacker agents to iteratively test and evade policy enforcement in both commercial and open-source LLMs and uncovers previously unknown model-specific vulnerabilities and attack-surface patterns.

catch_all_data.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into sensitive workflows, raising the stakes for adversarial robustness and safety. This paper introduces Transient Turn Injection (TTI), a new multi-turn attack technique that systematically exploits stateless moderation by distributing adversarial intent across isolated interactions. TTI leverages automated attacker agents to iteratively test and evade policy enforcement in both commercial and open-source LLMs and uncovers previously unknown model-specific vulnerabilities and attack-surface patterns.

transient_turn_injection_exposing_statel.arxiv_title: Transient Turn Injection: Exposing Stateless Multi-Turn Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models

breaking_mcp_function_hijacking_attacks_.description:
[arXiv] Breaking MCP with Function Hijacking Attacks: Novel Threats for Function Calling and Agentic Models

This paper introduces a novel function hijacking attack (FHA) that manipulates the tool-selection process of agentic models to force invocation of an attacker-chosen function. The attack is largely agnostic to context semantics and robust to the function sets, and can be trained into universal adversarial functions that hijack tool selection across multiple queries and payload configurations.

catch_all_data.abstract: This paper introduces a novel function hijacking attack (FHA) that manipulates the tool-selection process of agentic models to force invocation of an attacker-chosen function. The attack is largely agnostic to context semantics and robust to the function sets, and can be trained into universal adversarial functions that hijack tool selection across multiple queries and payload configurations.

breaking_mcp_function_hijacking_attacks_.arxiv_title: Breaking MCP with Function Hijacking Attacks: Novel Threats for Function Calling and Agentic Models

evaluating_answer_leakage_robustness_llm.description:
[arXiv] Evaluating Answer Leakage Robustness of LLM Tutors against Adversarial Student Attacks

This work studies adversarial students trying to extract final answers from LLM tutors. It adapts six groups of adversarial and persuasive techniques to the educational setting and introduces a fine-tuned adversarial student agent that jailbreaks tutor models as a standardized robustness benchmark.

catch_all_data.abstract: This work studies adversarial students trying to extract final answers from LLM tutors. It adapts six groups of adversarial and persuasive techniques to the educational setting and introduces a fine-tuned adversarial student agent that jailbreaks tutor models as a standardized robustness benchmark.

evaluating_answer_leakage_robustness_llm.arxiv_title: Evaluating Answer Leakage Robustness of LLM Tutors against Adversarial Student Attacks

jailbreaking_large_language_models_moral.description:
[arXiv] Jailbreaking Large Language Models with Morality Attacks

The paper develops morality-aware jailbreak attacks to manipulate LLM judgment on value-ambiguity and value-conflict questions. It formalizes four adversarial attacks over a 10.3K-instance morality dataset and shows critical vulnerability of both LLMs and guardrail models to subtle moral framing attacks.

catch_all_data.abstract: The paper develops morality-aware jailbreak attacks to manipulate LLM judgment on value-ambiguity and value-conflict questions. It formalizes four adversarial attacks over a 10.3K-instance morality dataset and shows critical vulnerability of both LLMs and guardrail models to subtle moral framing attacks.

jailbreaking_large_language_models_moral.arxiv_title: Jailbreaking Large Language Models with Morality Attacks

into_gray_zone_domain_contexts_can_blur_.description:
[arXiv] Into the Gray Zone: Domain Contexts Can Blur LLM Safety Boundaries

This paper proposes Jargon, a framework that combines safety-research contexts with multi-turn adversarial interactions to exploit gray-zone behavior in refusal systems. The method reportedly achieves very high attack success rates across frontier models by selectively relaxing defenses through domain and research framing.

catch_all_data.abstract: This paper proposes Jargon, a framework that combines safety-research contexts with multi-turn adversarial interactions to exploit gray-zone behavior in refusal systems. The method reportedly achieves very high attack success rates across frontier models by selectively relaxing defenses through domain and research framing.

into_gray_zone_domain_contexts_can_blur_.arxiv_title: Into the Gray Zone: Domain Contexts Can Blur LLM Safety Boundaries

templatefuzz_finegrained_chat_template_f.description:
[arXiv] TEMPLATEFUZZ: Fine-Grained Chat Template Fuzzing for Jailbreaking and Red Teaming LLMs

TEMPLATEFUZZ systematically mutates chat-template elements to expose an underexplored jailbreak surface. It combines element-level mutation rules, heuristic search, and an active-learning oracle to drive prompt and template variants toward higher attack success while preserving task accuracy.

catch_all_data.abstract: TEMPLATEFUZZ systematically mutates chat-template elements to expose an underexplored jailbreak surface. It combines element-level mutation rules, heuristic search, and an active-learning oracle to drive prompt and template variants toward higher attack success while preserving task accuracy.
